hero.headline: Kurukshetra University Online MBA in Marketing: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved 2-year programme | NAAC A++ accredited | Public state university | Rs 1,02,000 total fee

sections.tldr: Kurukshetra University Online MBA in Marketing covers Advertising Management, Marketing Research and Analytics, Sales and Logistics Management, Consumer Behaviour, Strategic Brand Management, Digital and Social Media Marketing, International Marketing, Business Marketing, Service Marketing, Strategic Marketing, and Rural and Agribusiness Marketing. Total fee Rs 1,02,000 (Rs 25,500/semester). NAAC A++ accredited public state university. Zero-cost EMI available directly through the university.

about.body:
Kurukshetra University's Online MBA in Marketing blends traditional marketing (advertising, sales, consumer behaviour, brand management) with digital-era content (digital and social media marketing, marketing research and analytics) and niche domains (rural and agribusiness marketing, service marketing). At Rs 1,02,000 total, this is the only NAAC A++ public state university offering a structured online MBA in Marketing at this fee tier.

The curriculum breadth is notable. Sem 3 alone has six specialisation subjects, including Strategic Brand Management and Digital and Social Media Marketing as standalone papers. Sem 4 adds five more, with Rural and Agribusiness Marketing covering a segment most online MBAs ignore entirely. For candidates targeting FMCG, agri-business, or rural distribution roles, this subject adds genuine differentiation.

Kurukshetra University is a state university established in 1956 under the Haryana government. NAAC A++ (the highest possible grade), UGC-DEB approved for online programmes. As a public university, tuition is government-subsidized, which explains the lower fee compared to private online MBA providers.

EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1-2 cover the common MBA core. Semesters 3-4 below show the Marketing specialisation subjects plus common core papers (Strategic Management, Optimization Models, Summer Training in Sem 3; Entrepreneurship Development, Business Ethics and Corporate Governance, Comprehensive Viva-Voce in Sem 4).

subjects.name: Strategic Management
subjects.desc: Common core. Competitive strategy frameworks, industry analysis, corporate-level strategy, and strategic planning process.

subjects.name: Optimization Models for Business Decisions
subjects.desc: Common core. Linear programming, transportation models, assignment problems, and quantitative decision-making tools.

subjects.name: Summer Training/Project Work
subjects.desc: Common core. Industry-based project applying management concepts to a real business problem.

subjects.name: Advertising Management
subjects.desc: Campaign planning, media selection, creative strategy, advertising budgets, and campaign effectiveness measurement.

subjects.name: Marketing Research and Analytics
subjects.desc: Research design, sampling methods, survey instruments, data analysis techniques, and marketing analytics tools.

subjects.name: Sales and Logistics Management
subjects.desc: Sales force design, territory management, distribution channel strategy, logistics operations, and supply chain coordination.

subjects.name: Consumer Behaviour
subjects.desc: Buyer decision process, psychological influences on purchase, cultural and social factors, and consumer segmentation models.

subjects.name: Strategic Brand Management
subjects.desc: Brand equity measurement, brand architecture, positioning strategy, brand extension decisions, and brand revitalization.

subjects.name: Digital and Social Media Marketing
subjects.desc: SEO, SEM, social media strategy, content marketing, influencer marketing, email campaigns, and digital marketing analytics.

subjects.name: Entrepreneurship Development
subjects.desc: Common core. Startup ideation, business plan creation, funding strategies, and entrepreneurial ecosystem in India.

subjects.name: Business Ethics and Corporate Governance
subjects.desc: Common core. Ethical frameworks, corporate social responsibility, board governance, and regulatory compliance.

subjects.name: Comprehensive Viva-Voce
subjects.desc: Common core. Oral examination covering all four semesters of the MBA programme.

subjects.name: International Marketing
subjects.desc: Market entry strategies, global pricing, cross-cultural marketing, export procedures, and international distribution channels.

subjects.name: Business Marketing
subjects.desc: B2B marketing strategy, organizational buying behaviour, key account management, and industrial product marketing.

subjects.name: Service Marketing
subjects.desc: Service quality frameworks (SERVQUAL), services pricing, service blueprinting, customer experience management, and service recovery.

subjects.name: Strategic Marketing
subjects.desc: Marketing strategy formulation, competitive positioning, market-driven planning, and marketing resource allocation.

subjects.name: Rural and Agribusiness Marketing
subjects.desc: Rural consumer behaviour, agri-product distribution, FMCG rural strategies, rural media, and agricultural value chain marketing.

whoHires.body:
Kurukshetra University MBA Marketing graduates target roles across FMCG, retail, digital marketing, and agribusiness:

**Industries:** FMCG, retail, e-commerce, advertising agencies, digital marketing, agribusiness, media and entertainment, pharmaceutical marketing, and B2B sales

**Entry-level roles:** Marketing Executive, Brand Coordinator, Digital Marketing Analyst, Market Research Analyst, Sales Executive, Content Marketing Associate

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Brand Manager, Digital Marketing Manager, Sales Manager, Trade Marketing Manager, Market Research Manager, E-Commerce Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Marketing, Head of Brand, CMO (mid-market), Head of Digital, National Sales Head, Director of Rural Marketing

**Employers:** FMCG companies (HUL, ITC, Nestlé, Dabur), retail chains, e-commerce platforms, advertising agencies, agribusiness firms, and Haryana/NCR regional corporates.

**Note:** As a public state university with NAAC A++, the Kurukshetra MBA degree carries strong credibility for government and PSU marketing/sales roles. Rural and Agribusiness Marketing adds differentiation for FMCG rural distribution careers.

skills.body:
The Marketing specialisation builds skills across brand management, digital marketing, research, and rural marketing:

**Technical skills:**
- Digital Marketing: SEO/SEM campaign setup, social media strategy design, content calendar management, Google Analytics interpretation, and paid ad optimization
- Marketing Research: survey design, sampling methodology, SPSS/Excel-based data analysis, conjoint analysis, and market sizing
- Brand Management: brand equity measurement (Aaker model), positioning map creation, brand architecture planning, and brand health tracking
- Advertising: media plan creation, creative brief writing, campaign ROI calculation, and programmatic advertising basics

**Applied skills:**
- Rural and Agribusiness Marketing: rural distribution network design, agri-product pricing, rural consumer profiling, and mandi-based supply chain analysis
- International Marketing: market entry mode evaluation, export documentation, cross-cultural communication, and global pricing strategy

**Soft skills:** Presenting campaign strategies to leadership, negotiating with media vendors, managing agency relationships, and pitching brand concepts to cross-functional teams.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Kurukshetra University Online MBA Marketing against peer programmes:

**vs Vignan Online MBA Marketing (Rs 90,000):** Vignan is Rs 12,000 cheaper with NAAC A+ and NIRF #70. Vignan covers retail management and CRM. Kurukshetra at Rs 1,02,000 is a NAAC A++ public university with 11 specialisation subjects, including Rural and Agribusiness Marketing and Strategic Brand Management as standalones. Kurukshetra wins on accreditation grade, public university status, and curriculum breadth.

**vs SMU Online MBA Marketing (Rs 1,20,000):** SMU is Rs 18,000 more expensive with NAAC A+ and Manipal brand. SMU has an established online platform and placement network. Kurukshetra is cheaper, holds NAAC A++, and adds rural/agribusiness marketing depth. SMU wins on brand. Kurukshetra wins on fee, accreditation, and niche curriculum coverage.

**vs Galgotias Online MBA Marketing (Rs 86,400):** Galgotias is Rs 15,600 cheaper with NAAC A+ and NCR location. Both cover digital marketing and consumer behaviour. Kurukshetra adds Rural and Agribusiness Marketing and has a higher NAAC grade. Pay more at Kurukshetra for the A++ accreditation and public university degree.

**Key differentiator:** Kurukshetra is the only NAAC A++ public university in this fee tier. Rural and Agribusiness Marketing is a rare standalone subject not found in most competitor programmes.

fees.body:
The Kurukshetra University Online MBA in Marketing is priced at Rs 1,02,000 total program fee across 4 semesters (2-year programme). As a NAAC A++ accredited public state university, Kurukshetra offers government-backed education at significantly lower fees than most private online MBA programmes.

**Payment options**:
- **Semester-wise**: Rs 25,500 per semester x 4 installments
- **Full upfront**: Rs 1,02,000 one-time

**Additional charges**: Specific registration, application, examination, or project fees are not separately documented on the Kurukshetra Online portal. Please reconfirm all charges with our counsellor before enrolment.

**Zero-cost EMI**: Available directly through Kurukshetra University (no external marketing partner or NBFC intermediary). Contact our counsellor for current tenure options.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Kurukshetra University each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees and all applicable charges with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive an MBA degree certificate with Marketing as the specialisation, issued by Kurukshetra University under UGC-DEB online mode standards.

The degree carries NAAC A++ accreditation (the highest possible grade) and UGC-DEB approval. As a public state university degree, it holds strong validity for government recruitment, PSU marketing/sales roles, and private-sector brand management positions. DigiLocker issuance is standard.

reviews.intro: Three reviews from Kurukshetra University Online MBA Marketing students. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Pooja T.
items.liked: A public state university MBA at Rs 1,02,000 with NAAC A++. The government-backed degree holds genuine credibility for government recruitment and PSU interviews
items.disliked: Online platform is functional but not as polished as Amity or Manipal. Could use a better mobile experience
items.body: I chose Kurukshetra because no private university matches NAAC A++ at this price. The marketing syllabus covers everything from digital marketing to rural agribusiness, which is rare. The public university tag matters for state government applications in Haryana. The LMS does the job. Not flashy, but the degree quality and fee combination are unmatched.

items.name: Rahul D.
items.liked: Rural and Agribusiness Marketing as a standalone paper is unique. I work in FMCG distribution and this subject directly applies to my role
items.disliked: Kurukshetra is respected in Haryana and Punjab but brand recognition weakens in South and West India. Employers there may not immediately recognise the university
items.body: The marketing curriculum has 11 specialisation subjects across Sem 3-4. That is broader than most competitors at this price. Digital and Social Media Marketing and Strategic Brand Management were my best subjects. The regional brand limitation is real. In North India the Kurukshetra name carries weight. For pan-India roles, you may need to explain the NAAC A++ public university positioning.

items.name: Sunita M.
items.liked: Rs 25,500 per semester is genuinely affordable. The zero-cost EMI helped me manage finances during the programme
items.disliked: Some recorded lectures need updating. A few marketing case studies referenced 2018-2019 campaigns. Faculty engagement in live sessions is inconsistent
items.body: At this price from a NAAC A++ public university, the value equation is clear. The syllabus reads well. Delivery quality varies by subject. Consumer Behaviour and Brand Management had strong faculty. Some other subjects had generic recorded content. Career services for online students are basic. If you need hand-held placement support, look at larger private universities. If you need an affordable, credible degree, Kurukshetra delivers.

faqs.question: Is Kurukshetra a public or private university?
faqs.answer: Kurukshetra University is a public state university established under the Haryana government. Public universities receive government funding, which keeps tuition lower than private institutions. This also means the degree carries state university recognition for government and PSU recruitment.

faqs.question: Why is the Kurukshetra Online MBA cheaper than Amity or NMIMS?
faqs.answer: Because Kurukshetra is a government-funded public state university. Private universities like Amity (Rs 3L+) and NMIMS (Rs 2.5L+) set their own fee structures. Public universities receive state subsidies that keep tuition significantly lower. Kurukshetra at Rs 1,02,000 total reflects this public university pricing model.

faqs.question: Does Kurukshetra offer zero-cost EMI?
faqs.answer: Yes. Zero-cost EMI is available directly through Kurukshetra University. No external NBFC or marketing partner is involved. Contact our counsellor for the current EMI tenure and monthly amount.

faqs.question: Does the Marketing MBA cover digital marketing and social media?
faqs.answer: Yes. Digital and Social Media Marketing is a standalone Sem 3 subject covering SEO, SEM, social media strategy, content marketing, influencer marketing, and digital analytics. It is a full-credit paper, not a module within a broader course.

faqs.question: Is Rural and Agribusiness Marketing a separate subject or a module?
faqs.answer: It is a standalone Sem 4 subject covering rural consumer behaviour, agri-product distribution, FMCG rural strategies, and agricultural value chain marketing. Most online MBA programmes at this fee tier do not offer rural marketing as a separate paper.

chooseIf: You want a NAAC A++ public state university degree. No other online MBA at this fee tier matches this combination.
Rural and Agribusiness Marketing as a standalone subject matters for your FMCG or agribusiness career.
Government job eligibility and PSU recruitment credibility are priorities. Public university degrees carry inherent weight.
Rs 1,02,000 total (Rs 25,500/semester) fits your budget with zero-cost EMI directly through the university.
consider: National brand recognition matters more than accreditation grade. Amity, NMIMS, and MUJ have stronger pan-India MBA brand positioning.
Vignan at Rs 90,000 is Rs 12,000 cheaper with NAAC A+ and NIRF #70, with retail management and CRM depth.
Galgotias at Rs 86,400 is the cheapest NAAC A+ option in the NCR region.
If premium LMS, polished delivery, and active placement support are non-negotiable, budget for a higher-fee private university programme.
